value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags American Electric Power Company, Inc.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-3HK3-RX9M-00JS-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags American Electric Power Company, Inc.2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags American Electric Power Company, Inc.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 8.4% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 41.5% or 14.9% annualized.

In comparison, American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 14.1% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 62.7% or 21.1% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $146.73 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 20.8. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.49 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s last trading price was $134.66 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 20.1. With an EPS growth rate of 14.1 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 1.43 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) is a regulated electric utility serving approximately 5.6 million customers across 11 U.S. states. The company generates the largest portion of its revenue from retail electric sales within its Vertically Integrated Utilities and Transmission & Distribution Utilities segments. In 2025, these segments contributed significantly to the $21.88 billion in total revenues, with regulated rates on residential, commercial, and industrial loads forming the core. The fastest-growing area was commercial retail within Transmission & Distribution, which saw a 27.8% year-to-date increase, driven by demand from data centers and hyperscalers. Incremental transmission investments and wholesale activities further supported overall load expansion.

value.Overview: Global Round Up - Bullish and Bearish Signals, 2862words

urn:contentItem:6JHK-Y103-S8YC-S4YF-00000-00Will Dover Corporation's (NYSE:DOV), recent surge keep climbing?2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:06ZLexisNexisWill Dover Corporation's (NYSE:DOV), recent surge keep climbing?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation has shown some strength in recent days. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, lifted 1.7% during its previous trading session to close at $227.18.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags Exelon Corporation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHN4-4267-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags Exelon Corporation2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:29ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Lags Exelon Corporation

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 8.4% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 41.5% or 14.9% annualized.

In comparison, Exelon Corporation's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 9.6% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 20.7% or 9.7% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $146.73 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 20.8. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.49 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Exelon Corporation's last trading price was $46.61 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 17. With an EPS growth rate of 9.6 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 1.77 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Exelon Corporation operates as a holding company for regulated electric and gas utilities, primarily generating revenue through electricity transmission and distribution, which accounts for approximately 85% of total revenue. The largest revenue contributors are its PHI subsidiary, focused on Mid-Atlantic operations, and ComEd, serving northern Illinois, which together represent over 58% of the company's $24.3 billion full-year revenue. The fastest-growing segments include BGE, driven by Maryland distribution rates, and PECO, supported by Pennsylvania electric and gas rates and favorable weather conditions. These segments have shown significant year-over-year growth, reflecting the company's investments in infrastructure and regulatory recoveries.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-Y673-SJ9G-V1WC-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with WEC Energy Group, Inc *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with WEC Energy Group, Inc *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, WEC Energy Group, Inc's trailing twelve months dividend is $3.63. Its dividend yield at its current price is 3.08%. Its current price is at a 1.4% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 20.1% premium to its 52 week low. If WEC Energy Group, Inc was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.7%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into WEC Energy Group, Inc stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,688 today. This total gain of $688 is generated by a capital gain of $457 and dividends of $231.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

WEC Energy Group, Inc's dividend has grown 7% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

value.Title: Garden River First Nation solar project shows path forward for Ontario 's grid
value.Overview: 8:30 AM EST CBC RADIO ONE PIYA CHATTOPADHYAY Mark Winfield The Current8:30 AM ESTCBC RADIO ONE, 1902words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-KPJ3-S0PB-P1DG-00000-00Garden River First Nation solar project shows path forward for Ontario's grid2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexis8:30 AM ESTCBC RADIO ONEPIYA CHATTOPADHYAYMark WinfieldTC2605014Garden River First Nation solar project shows path forward for Ontario's grid

PIYA CHATTOPADHYAY (HOST):

Though Canada still doesn't have a clean electricity strategy, that hasn't stopped provinces from approving renewable projects, like the solar farm the Garden River First Nation is planning. It's about 55 kilometers east of Sault Ste. Marie in Ontario. It is supposed to feed enough power into the province's grid for 40,000 homes. Evan Belleau is with the Garden Rivers Community Development Department.



EVAN BELLEAU: We're a part of this at the beginning and it's an opportunity to drive development in a way that's beneficial and long-term for us and our people. I think everybody understands the difficult task of balancing political whims of different geographic locations of the country. For us here to be able to contribute in a project that is using the power from the sun, the thing that gives us energy, the thing provides us with food and provides us with life, I think it's very important.

PC: Even though provinces are making investments in renewable energy projects, as you just heard, Canada is still lagging behind others and is last in the G7 countries. Mark Winfield is a professor of environmental and urban change and he's co-chair of the Sustainable Energy Initiative at York University in Toronto. Mark, good morning to you.

MARK WINFIELD: Good morning.

PC: So, we just heard solar and wind energy generates just under 9% of electricity in Canada. The G7 average is 19%. From your perspective, why is Canada lagging behind?

MARK WINFIELD: The problem in Canada has been an extremely high level of policy instability. We've gone from times when provinces have pursued quite aggressive strategies around renewable energy development in Ontario, for example, and then in Alberta as well. And then we've had those same provinces slam on the brakes virtually overnight. And again, we saw this in Ontario in 2018. We literally had wind turbines ripped out of the ground. And then more recently in Alberta, which had become the leader in renewable energy development, we the Smith government slam on the brakes and adopt a set of rules which make renewable development almost impossible.

PC: So, change in government means change in policy, huh?

MARK WINFIELD: That's what's happened is we've seen quite dramatic shifts with changes in government, although Ontario had gone through a very rapid period of build out between about 2009 and 2018, but 8000 megawatts of wind and solar from a standing start of zero. But even the wind government had been starting to slow down. But then there was a complete and rapid halt with the arrival of the Ford government of Ontario in June of 2018.

PC: He's sort of the leader provincially in terms of making headway when it comes to making these investments.

MARK WINFIELD: At the moment, it's hard to point to anybody in particular. Manitoba, interesting under the Canoe government, has begun to make some interesting progress on wind, working with Indigenous and Métis communities. Quebec does have a large commitment to wind development to complement its hydroelectric assets. And Nova Scotia has been talking about offshore wind, although none of that is really realized at this stage.

PC: What is the opportunity here for Canada when it comes to solar and wind energy?

MARK WINFIELD: Well, the opportunity is at one level almost infinite in terms of the potential for wind and solar, given geography, available wind resources, land is available, but keeping in mind that these are not exclusionary land uses. So, we have models in places like Ontario and to some degree Alberta moving in the interaction of systems that rely on combinations of hydroelectricity and renewables. Along with demand-side measures, distributed energy resources, and very substantial energy storage resources as well.

PC: So, you mentioned Alberta that was leading the charge when it came to renewables and then that stalled after the province imposed a seven-month moratorium on all projects about three years ago. Now the federal government and the provinces are in talks about a potential new oil pipeline. How should Canada balance investments in something like a pipeline with renewable energy.

MARK WINFIELD: Well, I think we have to look at this in a geopolitical context and recognize, as the previous guest was doing, that the combination of climate change and geopolitical instability around fossil fuel resources as a result of the war in Ukraine and now the war and the Persian Gulf in the Middle East is actually causing countries to pull back from fossil fuels. Precisely because of the geopolitical risk, and we've seen this very clearly in the case of the European Union, but also China as well. That's no accident that these have become the leaders because they're looking at both the climate question and the relationship between climate change and fossil fuels, but the geopolitics as well, so I think we to ask some hard questions about in the longer term. Where markets for fossil fuels are going, particularly given that Canada's likely exports, which is oil sands bitumen and fracked liquid natural gas from BC, are relatively carbon expensive, carbon intensive, and also relatively high cost.

PC: There are, as you know, people in our country who say, look, this might be a longer-term strategy, but right now more oil needs to get to market. We are an oil producing country and that's why Canada needs to build a pipeline to get its oil to markets across the Pacific and we just saw yesterday with Donald Trump approving a revived Keystone XL, different routing, but approving that. So what do you say to those arguments?

MARK WINFIELD: Well, I think we need to take a longer term perspective. The disruption in the Middle East is ultimately going to be temporary, I think, one way or another. Whereas the time frames for building pipelines are measured in years and even decades and also keeping in mind, particularly for oil, what we would be exporting is oil sands bitumen. For which the market is actually quite limited because it requires very specialized refining capacities. It's very carbon intensive and it's also quite expensive oil. So, in a world that in which the long-term demand is going to go down and where we're competing with places that they're still able to put a pipe in the ground and have liquid, petroleum, crude come out of it, I'm not sure it makes sense as a long- term investment or strategy. In climate terms, energy security terms, or economic terms and indeed, I think you see the reluctance of the private sector to invest in new pipeline capacity, I think is a pretty clear signal that there are very serious doubts about where the market would actually be going in the long term. We've talked about the provinces, let's talk.

PC: About the federal government. What do you see it should be playing right now and trying to, which you advocate for, pushing more renewable energy?

MARK WINFIELD: Well, at the moment, it's mostly doing almost nothing. The overwhelming focus, in terms of the federal government around Autristio, there have been some discussions around the National Grid and I think we saw hints at this. In the economic statement, there actually seems to be an overwhelming focus on nuclear, which is very strange, given that we're in the 40th anniversary of Chernobyl and that the economics of nuclear just seemed to get worse and worse. The closer and closer we get to real data and experience.

PC: Yeah, in that economic statement earlier this week, the federal government said it was planning to release a discussion paper to seek input from provinces and territories on how to modernize the grid. That's not far enough for your liking.

MARK WINFIELD: Well, I think it's helpful, but what needs to look at this, both at a national and regional level, I mean, I thin modernization of the grid is good and improving inter-jurisdictional interties is helpful, particularly between provinces, which on the one hand may have large storage capacity in the form of large hydroelectric assets, and other provinces, which may not have that much storage capacity because of accidents of geography. But may have very large renewable energy potential. There's been discussions around how to optimize Quebec's hydroelectric storage capacity within Eastern Canada or around relationships between Saskatchewan and Manitoba or BC and Alberta to sort of optimize the potential for renewable development in combination with the hydroelectric storage capacities that we do have in Canada.

PC: So, Mark, the federal government's clean electricity regulations that would come into effect in 2035 would gradually require all electricity generation to be from non-emitting sources by 2050. That's only 25 years from now. How confident are you, given where this country is, that that goal can be reached?

MARK WINFIELD: Well, I think confidence is probably shrinking, as was mentioned in the intro. The role of fossil fuel-fired generation in Canada is actually expanding significantly. Here in Ontario, it has quintupled since 2017. British Columbia is looking to expand fossil, so is New Brunswick. If anything, we're moving in the opposite direction. And we also are having some provinces, notably Saskatchewan, which are being quite explicit about their intention to defy the clean electricity regulations and the earlier coal phaseout and indeed, we also have to remember the federal government itself agreed to hold the clean-electricity regulations in abeyance through the memorandum of agreement that signed with Alberta last fall. So, the signals here are not very positive at all.

PC: I have to let you go, but let me ask you this before I do. We're in an unstable time. There's a lot of effort being put in to making new partnerships beyond the US, both economically and politically. What's at stake if Canada doesn't tap into the potential of renewables like solar and wind, like other countries are doing?

MARK WINFIELD: I think we're going to end up being left behind. We're going end up with something of a backwater, which is already happening because of the levels of instability we've experienced. But we're also putting ourselves on track to very, very carbon-intensive and very expensive electricity systems, which will undermine affordability, undermine competitiveness, and also undermine decarbonization, because nobody is going to decarbonize throughout electrification. If electricity costs are very, very high, which is where the nuclear and fossil pathway, for example, that we're seeing in Ontario, is inevitably going to take us.

PC: Mark, thank you for your insights this morning. Really appreciate it.

MARK WINFIELD: Thank you.

PC: Mark Winfield is a professor of environmental and urban change and co-chair of the Sustainable Energy Initiative at York University in Toronto. I'm Piya Chattopadhyay sitting in for Matt Galloway. You are listening to The Current. If you are a sports fan, tonight's a big night for Canadian hockey. The Montreal Canadiens are on the cusp of advancing to round two of the Stanley Cup Playoffs for the first time since 2021. The Habs are the only Canadian team left in this year's playoff. They need to just pull off one more win against the Tampa Bay Lightning and they have the chance to do that on home ice this evening. Here's the song Montreal fans love belting out along to at the Bell Center, especially when their team wins. Neil Diamond with Sweet Caroline.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-845H-00000-00DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) continues to underperform Alliant Energy Corporation by 4.8% in the past month2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) continues to underperform Alliant Energy Corporation by 4.8% in the past month

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's (NYSE:DTE) share price is continuing to underperform that of Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT). DTE Energy Company's return over the past month has been -5.1% and Alliant Energy Corporation's return has been -0.4% over the same period.

Here is how DTE Energy Company and Alliant Energy Corporation compare:

Company Name1 Month %3 Month%YTD %1 yr %DTE Energy Company-5.1-1.311.010.5Alliant Energy Corporation-0.42.313.023.3

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation operates as an energy-services provider focused on delivering regulated electricity and natural gas across the Midwest region. The core business centers on providing reliable energy solutions to end markets consisting of approximately 1,010,000 electric customers and 435,000 natural gas customers. The largest revenue sources stem from electric utility operations, which contributed $888 million of the $1,184 million in total first-quarter revenues, followed by natural gas utility revenues which generated $271 million. The fastest-growing segments are currently driven by significant momentum in data center growth and robust large-load customer demand. This ongoing rapid business expansion is supported by new electric service agreements that bring total contracted data center demand to approximately 3.4 GW, driving strategic infrastructure investments in generation and energy storage capacities.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's (NYSE:DTE) share price is continuing to underperform that of Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL). DTE Energy Company's return over the past month has been -5.1% and Xcel Energy Inc.'s return has been -3.0% over the same period.

Here is how DTE Energy Company and Xcel Energy Inc. compare:

Company Name1 Month %3 Month%YTD %1 yr %DTE Energy Company-5.1-1.311.010.5Xcel Energy Inc.-3.0-1.39.020.7

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy Inc. is a regulated utility providing electric and natural gas service to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across multiple states in the Upper Midwest and West. The company focuses its core business on delivering essential energy resources to these diverse end markets. The largest revenue source remains the electric segment, which generated $2,976 million, followed by natural gas revenues of $1,030 million. The fastest-growing sections of the organization include electric sales, infrastructure investments, recovery through non-fuel riders, and large-load opportunities sparked by major deals like the new Google data center agreement. Weather-normalized retail electric sales grew 2.8% year-over-year, driven primarily by a 4.3% increase in commercial and industrial sales, alongside steady customer growth across all of the utility's regulated operating territories and sectors.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S8GP-012B-00000-00ICYMI: Wednesday's Preview: Can Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), rebound after being down?2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:06ZLexisNexisICYMI: Wednesday's Preview: Can Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), rebound after being down?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, dropped 1.5% during its previous trading session to close at $216.71.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 387 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, increased 0.1% during its previous trading session to close at $219.97.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 387 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, dropped 1.5% during its previous trading session to close at $216.71.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

urn:contentItem:6JJN-M7W3-S8VK-W2C3-00000-00ICYMI: Traders look to see if Dover Corporation's (NYSE:DOV), recent rise will continue2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:06ZLexisNexisICYMI: Traders look to see if Dover Corporation's (NYSE:DOV), recent rise will continue

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, increased 0.1% during its previous trading session to close at $219.97.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 387 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

value.Title: Absentee voting has started in the Maine primary. Here's what you need to know.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJD-RX93-RXY2-123C-00000-00Absentee voting has started in the Maine primary. Here's what you need to know.2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:14ZLexisNexisUS STATE GOVERNMENT NEWSAbsentee voting has started in the Maine primary. Here's what you need to know.Rachel Ohm

While in-person voters mark ballots behind her, poll worker Sue Magee loads absentee ballots into a voting machine in June 2022 at Hall-Dale Elementary School in Hallowell. (Joe Phelan/Staff Photographer)
Absentee voting is underway ahead of Maine's June 9 primaries. That means voters can start casting ballots in the races for U.S. Senate, U.S. House and governor, along with local races.


The Democratic primary for Senate has generated lots of interest both in Maine and nationally. The winner will take on U.S. Sen. Susan Collins in a November match-up that could determine which party controls the upper chamber in Washington, D.C.
But most of the heat was taken out of the race last month when Gov. Janet Mills suspended her campaign, all but ceding victory to political newcomer Graham Platner.
Meanwhile, the governor's race is wide open and features a crowded field of five Democrats and seven Republicans. With no clear front-runners, it's almost certain that ranked-choice voting will determine the winners.
Here are a few things to know about who's on the ballot and how to absentee vote.
WHO'S ON THE BALLOT?
The Democratic primary for U.S. Senate will feature Platner, a veteran and oyster farmer from Sullivan, and David Costello, a former state and federal government worker from Brunswick. 
Advertisement
Mills' name also will appear on the Democratic primary ballot since her decision to drop out of the race came after the March 31 deadline for candidates to have their names removed. Mills has yet to formally notify the secretary of state's office that she is withdrawing from the race, which means that, as of Friday, any votes cast for her will still be counted.
A candidate must formally withdraw for the secretary of state to prepare notices to be posted at polling places and distributed with absentee ballots to let people know a vote for that candidate won't be counted.
In the Republican Senate primary, Collins is the only candidate on the ballot.
The governor's race features 12 candidates. Those in the Democratic primary are: Shenna Bellows, Maine's secretary of state; Troy Jackson, former Maine Senate president; Angus King III, a renewable energy entrepreneur and the son of U.S. Sen. Angus King; Hannah Pingree, former speaker of the Maine House and daughter of U.S. Rep. Chellie Pingree; and Nirav Shah, former director of the Maine Center for Disease Control and Prevention.
Candidates in the Republican primary are: Jonathan Bush, a businessman and cousin of former President George W. Bush; Bobby Charles, a former U.S. assistant secretary of state; David Jones, a real estate broker; Garrett Mason, former Maine Senate majority leader; Owen McCarthy, a medical technology entrepreneur; Ben Midgley, a former fitness franchise executive; and Robert Wessels, a retail manager from the town of Paris.
State Sen. Jim Libby, R-Standish, ended his campaign after failing to qualify for clean elections funds. But like Mills, he has not formally withdrawn and will still be on the ballot.
Advertisement
In the 1st Congressional District, U.S. Rep. Chellie Pingree is running unopposed in the Democratic primary. Republicans Joshua Pietrowicz, a former Toyota sales and leasing consultant from New Gloucester, and Ron Russell, a retired U.S. Army veteran and consultant from Kennebunkport, are vying for their party's nomination.
And in the 2nd District, Democrats Joe Baldacci, a state senator from Bangor; Matt Dunlap, the state auditor and former secretary of state; Jordan Wood, a former congressional staffer; and Paige Loud, a social worker and graduate student, will compete in the primary. Former Gov. Paul LePage is the only Republican on the ballot.
All 186 seats in the Maine Legislature are up for grabs this year, though most primaries are uncontested. Voters will also decide school budgets and other local issues and races in June.
WHO CAN VOTE IN THE PRIMARIES?
Maine has had semi-open primaries since 2024, meaning that unenrolled voters can participate in the party primary of their choice. Voters who are enrolled in a party may only vote in that party's primaries.
Enrolled voters who want to unenroll or switch parties must do so by May 26 (15 days prior to the election) in order to cast a ballot under their new affiliation in this year's primaries. When a voter enrolls in a new party, they may not change their enrollment again for three months unless they move to a new municipality and establish a new voting residence there.
About one-third of Maine's 1 million registered voters are unenrolled, so they could have a big impact on the outcome in the primaries.
Advertisement
HOW DO I REQUEST AN ABSENTEE BALLOT?
Maine has same-day voter registration at the polls, but voters looking to register online or by mail must do so by May 19 in order to participate in the June primary. For those registering by mail, your town or city clerk's office should receive your registration by the close of business on the 19th. Online registrations should be completed before 5 p.m. that day.
Absentee ballots can be requested online through the Maine secretary of state's office or by submitting a paper application or making a telephone request with your municipal clerk.
Municipalities are also required to offer in-person absentee voting starting 30 days before an election, but days and times vary by community. Voters can pick up, complete and return their ballot all at once. You can find out more about the days and times your municipality offers in-person absentee voting by contacting your town office or city clerk's office.
The deadline to request an absentee ballot or vote in-person absentee is June 4.
HOW DO I RETURN MY ABSENTEE BALLOT?
Ballots must be returned by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Ballots can be returned in-person, via drop box or by mail. The Maine secretary of state's office has advised that voters returning their ballots by mail should put their ballots in the mail no later than seven days prior to the election.
HOW WILL RANKED-CHOICE VOTING FACTOR IN THIS ELECTION?
Ranked-choice voting, which was first used in statewide elections in Maine in 2018, allows voters to rank candidates in order of preference in races featuring three or more contenders.
Advertisement
If no candidate gets more than 50% support in the first round, then the last-place finisher is eliminated, the second-choice votes of that candidate's supporters are reallocated to the remaining candidates and the results are retabulated. The process repeats until a winning candidate receives more than 50% support.
Both primaries in the governor's race and the Democratic primary in the 2nd District are likely to be decided using multiple rounds of tabulation.
For voters who want to learn more about the process and strategy behind ranked-choice voting, there are two upcoming events that could be helpful.
On May 12, the conservative organization Lead Maine and the Maine Republican Party will lead a conversation on ranked-choice voting at 6:30 p.m. in Lewiston. (Location provided with RSVP online.)
The Portland Public Library and Maine secretary of state's office will also hold a nonpartisan mock ranked-choice election and informational session at the library's downtown branch, at 5 Monument Square, at 10:30 a.m. on Saturday, May 23.
