urn:contentItem:6JJF-GFY3-RW84-109D-00000-00DTE Energy Company continues to underperform Xcel Energy Inc. by 9.2% over the past year2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company continues to underperform Xcel Energy Inc. by 9.2% over the past year

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's (NYSE:DTE) share price is continuing to underperform that of Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL). DTE Energy Company's return over the past year has been 7.1% and Xcel Energy Inc.'s return has been 16.3% over the same period.

Here is how DTE Energy Company and Xcel Energy Inc. compare:

Company Name1 Month %3 Month%YTD %1 yr %DTE Energy Company-4.84.99.97.1Xcel Energy Inc.-0.46.09.716.3

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy Inc. is a regulated utility providing electric and natural gas service to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across multiple states in the Upper Midwest and West. The company focuses its core business on delivering essential energy resources to these diverse end markets. The largest revenue source remains the electric segment, which generated $2,976 million, followed by natural gas revenues of $1,030 million. The fastest-growing sections of the organization include electric sales, infrastructure investments, recovery through non-fuel riders, and large-load opportunities sparked by major deals like the new Google data center agreement. Weather-normalized retail electric sales grew 2.8% year-over-year, driven primarily by a 4.3% increase in commercial and industrial sales, alongside steady customer growth across all of the utility's regulated operating territories and sectors.

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $140.60), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has fell $8.19 (or 5.5%) in the past week to close at $140.60.

This makes it the thirty-first lowest performer in the S&P 500 Index which was up 2.3% in the past week; a relative price change of -7.8%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 5.8% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.7 billion to $29.2 billion.

This has been exacerbated by high volume of 1.9 times average for the week.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

Friday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 6th consecutive fall

DETROIT, Friday, May 08 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $140.57) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional $1.43 (1.0%) to close at $140.57. This brought its 6-day fall to 7.3%; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 187.6 points (2.6%) in the six days for a relative price change of -9.9%. The stock decreased 0.7% on Wednesday and 0.3% on Thursday. The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 6-day volume of 1.8 times the average 6-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,279,125 shares worth $179.8 million traded today.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $140.60), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has fell $8.19 (or 5.5%) in the past week to close at $140.60.

This makes it the thirty-first lowest performer in the S&P 500 Index which was up 2.3% in the past week; a relative price change of -7.8%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 5.8% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.6%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.7 billion to $29.2 billion.

This has been exacerbated by high volume of 1.9 times average for the week.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Financial News

Quarterly Report (Q1/2026):

Release Date: April 30, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced net profit of $247m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 64% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 44% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

Friday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) in 6th consecutive fall

DETROIT, Friday, May 08 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $140.57) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional $1.43 (1.0%) to close at $140.57. This brought its 6-day fall to 7.3%; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 187.6 points (2.6%) in the six days for a relative price change of -9.9%. The stock decreased 0.7% on Wednesday and 0.3% on Thursday. The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 6-day volume of 1.8 times the average 6-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,279,125 shares worth $179.8 million traded today.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

urn:contentItem:6JHT-X6B3-RSSR-R2S9-00000-00Friday's Preview: Can Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), rebound after being down?2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:06ZLexisNexisFriday's Preview: Can Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), rebound after being down?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, fell 2.8% during its previous trading session to close at $220.90.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 387 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Alliant Energy Corporation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-8873-SHNH-22J5-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Alliant Energy Corporation2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Alliant Energy Corporation

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been -0.1% annualized over 5 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 36.7% or 9.5% annualized.

In comparison, Alliant Energy Corporation's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 5% annualized over 5 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 48.6% or 11.4% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $143.50 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.2. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.66 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Alliant Energy Corporation's last trading price was $73.07 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23.2. With an EPS growth rate of 4.9 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 4.75 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation is a regulated utility holding company. It provides electric and natural gas services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Midwest. The company operates through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its primary revenue stream is electric utility operations, which constituted about 93% of third-quarter 2025 revenues. This segment is also its fastest-growing, with revenues up 13% year-over-year, driven by rate increases, higher sales volumes, and expanding data center demand. Gas utility revenues represent approximately 4% of the total, with other utility and non-utility segments comprising the rest. The gas segment also showed growth, increasing 4% amid seasonal fluctuations.

value.Title: Indra Energy 's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-N3N3-RSVB-V2S9-00000-00Indra Energy's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:02ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWSIndra Energy's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power

Houston, TX, United States, May 11th, 2026, FinanceWire

Indra Energy, a provider of renewable energy plans, has announced an expanded focus on improving access to renewable energy for homeowners amid evolving market conditions. The development comes as deregulated energy markets and increasing demand for clean energy continue to influence how consumers source electricity.

The company stated that it is positioning its renewable-backed energy plans to address barriers to entry that have historically limited homeowner participation in renewable energy programs. By leveraging existing market structures, Indra Energy aims to offer alternatives that may broaden access without requiring significant infrastructure changes at the household level.

In a recent interview, Jeff Rodgers, Chief Marketing Officer at Indra Energy, discussed the company's approach to making renewable energy options more widely available. He outlined how the company's plan offerings are designed to align with changing consumer preferences and regulatory environments.

The Power of Choice

For many years, the majority of homeowners haven't really had a choice about where they get their power. In many cities and states across the country, customers are restricted to a single electricity supplier that has an essential monopoly over this service. For homeowners who want to switch to "clean" power sources, installing rooftop solar panels is the only option.

Entering energy deregulation - a system that has seen growing adoption across the country to give homeowners the ability to choose their energy supplier. Unlike areas that have a regulated monopoly, deregulated markets allow a variety of suppliers to compete in a particular market. According to Rodgers, market deregulation is an important first step in expanding clean energy access.

"With deregulated energy markets, homeowners are in control of choosing their power supplier - and that includes having the option to use clean energy providers," he says. "Deregulated markets tend to lead the way in clean energy adoption simply because there isn't a utility monopoly that controls everything. When people have the ability to choose, those who prioritize clean energy are going to make that a priority in their supplier search."

To that end, Indra Energy has begun introducing plans for electricity and natural gas in parts of the Northeast and Midwest U.S. that offer deregulated markets. Residents of Delaware, Indiana, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia and the District of Columbia can receive electricity, natural gas or both utilities through Indra Energy.

No Solar Panels Required

Another long-standing belief regarding clean power is that it requires homeowners to install solar panels or other equipment to generate renewable energy on their own. While solar panels are growing in popularity, they are often inaccessible. A report by the National Renewable Energy Laboratory determined that 49% of households in the United States could not install a solar panel system because of insufficient roof space, insufficient roof access (such as in a multi-unit housing facility) or renting status.

Aside from these broad concerns, many homeowners simply can't afford to install solar panels because of financial constraints, while local building codes restricting panel placement can also limit energy generation capacity.

Rodgers and the Indra Energy team have opted for a simpler approach that streamlines clean energy access for homeowners. "Our electricity plans are backed by Renewable Energy Credits (RECs). These are issued to certify when one megawatt-hour of electricity has been generated by a renewable source like solar, wind or hydro," he explains."For our electric plans, Indra Energy purchases energy from RECs for the individual homeowners' energy usage. Similarly, our natural gas plans are paired with carbon offsets. We match natural gas use with verified offset projects designed to reduce or remove greenhouse gases so that you will have net-zero emissions from your energy use."Rodgers notes that this setup means homeowners aren't necessarily getting "green electrons" delivered directly to their home. "As an energy supplier, when we purchase RECs, it ensures that renewable energy is added to the grid in an amount that equals your usage. The electricity delivered to your home comes from the grid mix, which can include a mix of renewable and non-renewable sources. While you may not be directly getting renewables, you can have confidence that your usage has been matched with renewable energy, so you're still having a positive impact on the environment."

A Streamlined Way to Switch

With no additional equipment required, the process of switching to Indra Energy is surprisingly simple. When homeowners in deregulated markets select Indra Energy as their supplier, their electricity and natural gas continue to be delivered through existing utility lines.

While the supplier changes, the local utility still handles ongoing service, including system maintenance and repairs, meter reads and billing. Because energy is provided through existing lines, homeowners don't experience any interruption to their service.

"I hope this really changes the way people think about accessing renewable energy," Rodgers says. "It doesn't have to be this big expensive process that might not even work for your property. It can be as simple as signing up for a new energy provider and having access to clean energy, without any visible change to your home or daily routine. When accessibility becomes the standard, green energy adoption can increase dramatically and we can have a bigger collective impact on the environment."

As Rodgers and Indra Energy illustrate, how homeowners are accessing clean energy is rapidly changing. With more accessible solutions readily available, green energy is poised to expand beyond its relatively niche adoption rates into something that can be used by a broader range of homeowners than ever before.

About Indra Energy

Indra Energy is an independent, licensed supplier of renewable electricity and carbon-offset natural gas. Its residential energy plans help customers reduce their carbon footprint without installing solar panels by supplying electricity backed by Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) and offsetting all natural gas usage with carbon credits. Indra Energy currently offers plans across seven markets and 39 utilities in Delaware, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia and the District of Columbia.

Contact

Indra Energy

hello@indraenergy.com

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Xcel Energy Inc.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-8873-SHNH-22HK-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Xcel Energy Inc.2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 5-Year Earnings Growth Lags Xcel Energy Inc.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been -0.1% annualized over 5 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 36.7% or 9.5% annualized.

In comparison, Xcel Energy Inc.'s growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 4.3% annualized over 5 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 32.9% or 9% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $143.50 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.2. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.66 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Xcel Energy Inc.'s last trading price was $81.45 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23.7. With an EPS growth rate of 2.7 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 8.92 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy Inc. is a regulated utility providing electric and natural gas service to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across multiple states in the Upper Midwest and West. The company focuses its core business on delivering essential energy resources to these diverse end markets. The largest revenue source remains the electric segment, which generated $2,976 million, followed by natural gas revenues of $1,030 million. The fastest-growing sections of the organization include electric sales, infrastructure investments, recovery through non-fuel riders, and large-load opportunities sparked by major deals like the new Google data center agreement. Weather-normalized retail electric sales grew 2.8% year-over-year, driven primarily by a 4.3% increase in commercial and industrial sales, alongside steady customer growth across all of the utility's regulated operating territories and sectors.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-DPW3-RXW9-W0YC-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Xcel Energy Inc.2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Xcel Energy Inc.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 7.4% annualized over 10 years.

In comparison, Xcel Energy Inc.'s growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 5.9% annualized over 10 years.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $142.44 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.1. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.63 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Xcel Energy Inc.'s last trading price was $80.55 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23.4. With an EPS growth rate of 2.7 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 8.82 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy Inc. is a regulated utility providing electric and natural gas service to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across multiple states in the Upper Midwest and West. The company focuses its core business on delivering essential energy resources to these diverse end markets. The largest revenue source remains the electric segment, which generated $2,976 million, followed by natural gas revenues of $1,030 million. The fastest-growing sections of the organization include electric sales, infrastructure investments, recovery through non-fuel riders, and large-load opportunities sparked by major deals like the new Google data center agreement. Weather-normalized retail electric sales grew 2.8% year-over-year, driven primarily by a 4.3% increase in commercial and industrial sales, alongside steady customer growth across all of the utility's regulated operating territories and sectors.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DOWNERS GROVE, Thursday, May 07 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Dover Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV), the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market cap, fell 2.8% during its previous trading session to close at $220.90.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) is a diversified global manufacturer with a robust presence in industrial heat exchangers, notably through its SWEP brazed plate heat exchangers, which provide high-efficiency heat transfer solutions for HVAC, refrigeration, and district heating applications. The company leverages innovative compact brazed plate technology to deliver small footprint and energy-efficient heat exchangers, catering to thermal management needs across sectors. Dover's strategic partnerships further enhance its competitive edge in heat transfer solutions. The company's ongoing commitment to technology leadership fosters sustainable growth through custom heat exchanger design and renewable energy applications, solidifying its standing in the market.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Guild Garage Group Expands with Dover & Company Partnership

Guild Garage Group has announced a significant partnership with Dover & Company, a prominent garage door service provider based in Flint, Michigan. This collaboration marks a pivotal moment for Guild as it expands its footprint into the Michigan market, with the announcement coming on 27th April 2026. Dover becomes Guild's 30th acquisition since the company was launched in 2024, highlighting its rapid growth trajectory in the sector.

Tim O'Reilly, CEO of Guild Garage Group, expressed enthusiasm about the new partnership, stating, "Entering the Michigan market marks an exciting milestone for us as we welcome our 30th partner into the Guild family. This partnership reflects our continued commitment to strategic growth and to aligning with organizations that share our values, culture, and dedication to service." O'Reilly's comments underscore the company's focus on building strong alliances while respecting the core values of its partners, such as those exemplified by Dover.

Dover & Company, founded in 1935, is celebrated for its commitment to quality service and reliability, having developed a strong reputation over nearly a century. Chuck Richards, the owner of Dover, echoed sentiments of mutual respect, stating, "From the very first conversations, I felt that the Guild Team showed genuine respect for the history and core values that have shaped Dover over the years. Their goal wasn't to transform our identity but to reinforce it." This partnership is viewed as a means to leverage Guild's resources while maintaining Dover's long-standing commitment to its customers.

Guild Garage Group continues to seek partnerships with leading garage door service businesses across the nation, emphasizing its strategy to invest in companies with robust management teams and cultures. This approach not only provides financial opportunities for business owners but also enhances service capabilities for customers. Interested parties can reach out to Bennet Crosby at Guild for potential collaboration opportunities.

As Guild Garage Group solidifies its position in the industry with this new acquisition, the company remains focused on creating unmatched growth opportunities for its partners. By allowing owners to retain unit-level ownership, Guild ensures that they continue to benefit from their business's success while expanding their operational capabilities and community impact.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 387 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: SWEP Partners with MCD to Broaden Heat Transfer Solutions

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., SWEP, part of Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) and a global leader in brazed plate heat exchangers (BPHEs), announced a strategic partnership with Microchannel Devices (MCD) to expand its offerings in printed circuit heat exchangers (PCHE) on 21st April 2026. This collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing SWEP's market presence and product range, as both companies focus on delivering high-performance heat exchangers that optimize efficiency and support sustainable energy solutions.

The partnership will enable SWEP to access MCD's cutting-edge printed circuit technology, which complements its existing brazed plate technology. According to Ulrika Nordqvist, SWEP President, this collaboration not only enhances SWEP's position in existing markets but also allows MCD to benefit from SWEP's extensive global sales channels and established customer relationships. The synergy between both companies underscores a strong commitment to advancing the green energy transition and addressing market needs in hydrogen infrastructure, energy storage, and CO2 applications.

Through a buy-resell agreement, customers of SWEP will gain access to a diverse portfolio of ultra-compact heat transfer solutions, ensuring seamless procurement processes and established communication channels. The expanded PCHE offerings will be supplied by MCD, an Italian specialist in chemical etching, known for its patented diffusion bonding processes and fully integrated design and production capabilities. This partnership is expected to significantly enhance the customer value proposition by providing solutions tailored to demanding applications characterized by space constraints and extreme operating conditions.

SWEP has been a pioneer in the heat transfer industry since 1983, providing innovative brazed plate heat exchangers that have been integrated into various HVACR and industrial applications worldwide. The collaboration with MCD is poised to further solidify SWEP's role in the global market, as it continues to expand its expertise in sustainable energy solutions. With a workforce of over 1,100 employees and a presence in 50 countries, SWEP is positioned to leverage its capabilities in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment of Dover Corporation.

Dover Corporation, headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois, operates as a diversified global manufacturer with an annual revenue exceeding $8 billion. The company focuses on delivering innovative equipment, components, and solutions across various sectors, including Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. The strategic partnership between SWEP and MCD is expected to enhance Dover's overall market offerings and its commitment to redefining possibilities in the energy sector.

Source: New York Stock Exchange: https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/stocks/DOV, company (Dover Corporation), Ticker(DOV)

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Dover Corporation, a diversified global manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in providing innovative industrial products and solutions across various sectors. Its core business activities include engineered components, fluid management systems, refrigeration and food equipment, digital printing technologies, and fueling solutions, serving industries such as manufacturing, energy, and consumer goods. The company operates through five main segments: Engineered Products, Fueling Solutions, Imaging, Pumps and Process Solutions, and Climate and Sustainability Technologies. Among these, the Climate and Sustainability Technologies segment has emerged as one of the fastest-growing, fueled by rising demand for energy-efficient and eco-friendly innovations. Additionally, advancements in digital solutions within Fueling Solutions and Imaging are experiencing accelerated growth, driven by trends in automation and e-commerce. This strategic diversification enables Dover to deliver reliable, high-performance products that support operational efficiency and sustainability for customers worldwide. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Industrial machinery company by market capitalisation.

Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) stands as a diversified global industrial powerhouse, excelling in the production of industrial pumps, including centrifugal pumps, positive displacement pumps, and metering pumps. It caters to various sectors with innovative solutions like sanitary food-grade pumps, chemical dosing pumps, and rotary lobe pumps. The firm’s expertise extends to crafting specialized peristaltic hose pumps and slurry solids-handling pumps, crucial for pumps for oil and gas. It provides comprehensive fluid handling systems, complemented by valves and actuators. Additionally, Dover’s portfolio includes heat exchangers and diverse pumps and process equipment. The packaging sector benefits from Dover's printing and coding equipment, featuring continuous inkjet printers, thermal inkjet coders, industrial labeling machines, and case coding and marking systems. In packaging machinery, its offerings include form-fill-seal machines, case packers, and palletizers. Their refrigeration and food equipment are optimal for commercial refrigeration units, cold storage solutions, and food processing equipment. Moreover, they supply fueling dispensers and systems, fuel management systems, industrial automation controls, and advanced sensors and transmitters.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-MNG3-RTGT-G0P8-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Alliant Energy Corporation

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 8.4% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 41.5% or 14.9% annualized.

In comparison, Alliant Energy Corporation's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 4.9% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 47.5% or 16.5% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $146.73 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 20.8. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.49 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Alliant Energy Corporation's last trading price was $73.68 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23.4. With an EPS growth rate of 4.9 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 4.79 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Alliant Energy Corporation is a regulated utility holding company. It provides electric and natural gas services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Midwest. The company operates through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its primary revenue stream is electric utility operations, which constituted about 93% of third-quarter 2025 revenues. This segment is also its fastest-growing, with revenues up 13% year-over-year, driven by rate increases, higher sales volumes, and expanding data center demand. Gas utility revenues represent approximately 4% of the total, with other utility and non-utility segments comprising the rest. The gas segment also showed growth, increasing 4% amid seasonal fluctuations.

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $142.44), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has decreased $4.59 (or 3.1%) in the past week to close at $142.44. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 229.2 points (or 3.2%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -6.3%. The volume was 2 times average weekly trading of 6.8 million shares.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 8.0% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.8%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $954.7 million to $29.6 billion.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

Financial News

Quarterly Report (Q1/2026):

Release Date: April 30, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: DTE Energy sequential quarterly profits hit by 64% fall

DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE), announced net profit of $247m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 64% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 44% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 44.4%, Revenue was up 15.8% and Net Profit was down 44.5%.

Wednesday's price drop

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) keeps sliding, down 6.1% in 4 days

DETROIT, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - DTE Energy Company's (NYSE: DTE $142.41) stock price extended its slide on Wednesday, shedding an additional $1.08 (0.8%) to close at $142.41. The shares have tumbled $9.28 (6.1%) over the past four trading days; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 152.7 points (2.1%) in the four days for a relative price change of -8.2%. The fall was exacerbated by a cumulative 4-day volume of 1.8 times the average 4-day volume of 1.4 million shares. There were 1,546,286 shares worth $220.2 million traded today. In the past five days trading has been on consistently high volumes; Volume Index (VI) has been 1.1 or higher.

SECTION 3 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

3.1 Uptrend

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.04:

- The Price/MAP 200 for DTE Energy Company is 1.04. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $149.93 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $136.56, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $136.56, a third bullish indicator.

3.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 11.9% versus sector average of 9.7%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 11.9% is better than sector average of 9.7%. This is computed as net profit margin of 9.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.29 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 4.4.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-5683-S8J5-X55B-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with American Electric Power Company, Inc. *Updated*2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with American Electric Power Company, Inc. *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $3.76. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.74%. Its current price is at a 0.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 44.1% premium to its 52 week low. If American Electric Power Company, Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.95%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into American Electric Power Company, Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,176 today. This total gain of $1,176 is generated by a capital gain of $849 and dividends of $326.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s dividend has grown 5.3% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) is a regulated electric utility serving approximately 5.6 million customers across 11 U.S. states. The company generates the largest portion of its revenue from retail electric sales within its Vertically Integrated Utilities and Transmission & Distribution Utilities segments. In 2025, these segments contributed significantly to the $21.88 billion in total revenues, with regulated rates on residential, commercial, and industrial loads forming the core. The fastest-growing area was commercial retail within Transmission & Distribution, which saw a 27.8% year-to-date increase, driven by demand from data centers and hyperscalers. Incremental transmission investments and wholesale activities further supported overall load expansion.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE $143.50), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, has decreased $5.08 (or 3.4%) in the past week to close at $143.50. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 120.4 points (or 1.7%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -5.1%.

The shares had their worst week in six weeks since the week-ended Mar 31, 2026.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 7.5% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.8%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.1 billion to $29.8 billion.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a diversified energy firm focusing on electric and natural gas services, primarily in Michigan. Key operations include DTE Electric, offering customers dependable power, and energy efficiency programs that enhance operational performance. DTE Energy is advancing in renewable energy with solar and wind projects and actively supports commercial solar initiatives. Its innovation pipeline includes smart grid modernization and battery storage, increasing energy management capabilities. Long-term competitive strengths are seen in its substantial investment in clean energy and DTE's commitment to community sustainability, ensuring robust corporate social responsibility through DTE Energy Foundation and sponsorships.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

DTE Energy Company, headquartered in Detroit, Michigan, is a diversified energy utility serving millions of customers across the Midwest United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through subsidiaries like DTE Electric and DTE Gas. The company operates a mix of energy sources, including coal, nuclear, and renewables, while focusing on reliable infrastructure and customer services for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors.

Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, such as wind and solar developments, which have expanded rapidly to meet rising demand for clean energy and regulatory mandates. Additionally, DTE Energy's non-utility operations, including energy trading and infrastructure services, are gaining momentum through investments in electric vehicle charging networks and advanced energy storage solutions. This strategic shift underscores the company's commitment to sustainability and long-term growth in a dynamic energy market. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) is a prominent player in the energy sector, offering a comprehensive range of services including DTE Energy electricity service and DTE Energy natural gas service. With a strong focus on sustainability, DTE Energy renewable energy initiatives are spearheaded by its DTE Energy solar programs and DTE Energy wind projects. The company is a leader in DTE Energy EV charging stations and is committed to DTE Energy grid modernization through innovations like DTE Energy smart meters and DTE Energy battery energy storage. Customers benefit from DTE Energy energy efficiency programs and a variety of DTE Energy rate plans. The company’s investment in DTE Energy community solar and DTE Energy commercial energy solutions aligns with its DTE Energy net zero strategy and DTE Energy carbon reduction goals. DTE Energy corporate sponsorship, including DTE sponsorship City Opera House, highlights its robust DTE Energy community partnerships, reinforcing DTE Energy utility services Michigan. DTE Energy investor NYSE:DTE supports its growth trajectory through DTE Energy business energy management and DTE Energy rebates and incentives.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Xcel Energy Inc.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-0MV3-SCBH-W0VJ-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Xcel Energy Inc.2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:05ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 3-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms Xcel Energy Inc.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 8.4% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 41.5% or 14.9% annualized.

In comparison, Xcel Energy Inc.'s growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 2.7% annualized over 3 years. Over the same time period its stock price has gone up 28.2% or 11.6% annualized.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $146.73 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 20.8. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.49 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Xcel Energy Inc.'s last trading price was $81.17 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 23.7. With an EPS growth rate of 2.7 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 8.91 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy Inc. is a regulated utility providing electric and natural gas service to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across multiple states in the Upper Midwest and West. The company focuses its core business on delivering essential energy resources to these diverse end markets. The largest revenue source remains the electric segment, which generated $2,976 million, followed by natural gas revenues of $1,030 million. The fastest-growing sections of the organization include electric sales, infrastructure investments, recovery through non-fuel riders, and large-load opportunities sparked by major deals like the new Google data center agreement. Weather-normalized retail electric sales grew 2.8% year-over-year, driven primarily by a 4.3% increase in commercial and industrial sales, alongside steady customer growth across all of the utility's regulated operating territories and sectors.

value.Title: Appeals court upholds key pieces of state permitting for big wind, solar projects Michigan local governments had legally challenged rules stripping them of final say over sprawling wind, solar and battery projects in some situations.
value.Overview: The Flint Journal: Web Edition (Michigan), ENVIRONMENT, 989words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHW-0193-RSKP-33DF-00000-00Appeals court upholds key pieces of state permitting for big wind, solar projects 2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:55:06ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENTAppeals court upholds key pieces of state permitting for big wind, solar projects Michigan local governments had legally challenged rules stripping them of final say over sprawling wind, solar and battery projects in some situations.Lucas Smolcic Larson

The Michigan Court of Appeals on Thursday upheld key aspects of state rules stripping local communities of final say over sprawling solar farms and other renewable energy projects that can blanket farmland.

In a split ruling, a panel of three judges preserved a finding from state regulators that local governments can't impose greater restrictions on the projects than those outlined in a controversial 2023 law giving some developers the option to skip over them and go to the state for approval.

However, the judges -- Michael Gadola, Christopher Murray and Michael Kelly -- agreed with some parts of a legal challenge mounted by 79 townships and counties to the rules, striking down two narrow provisions.

It is unclear how the opinion will affect nine solar and wind farms in the process of seeking state approval before the Michigan Public Service Commission.

But regulators on the commission, who drafted the rules at issue in the case and defended them before the appeals court, took the win.

"While the Commission continues to review the impact of specific findings of the Court's decision on cases before us, today's decision largely affirms the Commission's approach and allows for continued and timely implementation of the law," said Public Service Commission spokesperson Matt Helms in a statement.

Asked if a further appeal was coming, an attorney for the local governments said nothing was off the table at this time.

The lawyer, Michael Homier, said he and his clients were pleased with aspects of the decision, particularly where judges found regulators misinterpreted state law provisions. But they were also disappointed the court didn't agree with their arguments over how communities should be permitted to control projects with local zoning, he added.

Townships have chafed at the possibility of losing control over renewable energy permitting since Democrats passed the 2023 law, Public Act 233, over GOP opposition.

Supporters contend it was necessary to respond to a patchwork of local zoning rules across the state, some so restrictive that they gummed up valid projects for years. Without it, Michigan could never ramp up carbon-free power sources fast enough to meet state benchmarks and avoid the worst effects of climate change, they said.

After the law passed, it was up to the Public Service Commission, the regulators who would ultimately review project applications at the state level, to implement it.

They did so in an order outlining procedures for developers to follow in October 2024, before the law took effect at the end of November 2024. Local governments sued to appeal this order soon after.

The guidelines are complicated.

Generally, developers have to start at the local level, holding a public gathering and offering to meet with local officials. But when they face restrictive rules, permitting delays or outright denial, they can take their projects to the state for approval. This option only applies to solar, wind or battery storage projects over certain size thresholds.

In challenging the rules, townships and counties took issue with how regulators defined what the law calls a "compatible renewable energy ordinance."

If townships or counties have these local rules that align with minimum standards in the law for things like project setbacks, fencing, height and noise levels, they're deemed to have a compatible ordinance and can force developers to go through local permitting before resorting to state approval.

Regulators determined compatible ordinances could be no more restrictive than state standards and contain no additional requirements. Casting aside legal arguments to the contrary, the appeals court judges agreed in their 18-page ruling.

But they also threw out two other aspects of the state rules.

First, they rejected how the regulations defined an "affected local unit," or a local jurisdiction where a project will be located. Regulators sought to restrict this to only communities with zoning authority, but the judges found the law covers all local governments where the project will be physically located.

"It is a straightforward and simple definition to apply," they wrote in their ruling.

Being considered an affected local unit is important because it entitles communities to notice of public meetings about a project, the ability to legally intervene in state permitting and sizeable payments for hosting projects.

The appeals court also threw out a piece of the state rules that sets a timeline for when developers must apply for local approval if communities have a compatible ordinance in place.

Regulators said it should come 30 days after developers offer to meet with local elected officials, a requirement of the law. But judges ruled that was a misinterpretation, and the 30-day timer should start after the meeting itself, casting aside state attorneys' concerns that local governments could block projects by simply refusing to meet.

The judges upheld other portions of the state rules that the counties and townships had argued were unlawful. This included a definition of "hybrid" renewable energy projects that combine technologies like solar with grid-scale batteries.

Clean energy groups, including trade organizations for development companies that work in Michigan, celebrated the ruling in a statement issued after it was published.

The court "affirmed the ability for Michiganders to use their land as they wish while stimulating job creation and economic development," said Laura Sherman, president of the Michigan Energy Innovation Business Council.

Her organization had submitted briefs in support of the rules, joining environmental groups, a trade union, Democratic lawmakers and several landowners wishing to host projects. Meanwhile, the Michigan Farm Bureau, attorneys for the GOP-controlled Michigan House of Representatives and associations representing local governments supported the legal challenge to the regulations.

value.Title: DTE Energy 's dividend in perspective: comparison with Exelon Corporation *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-60M3-RWHH-2349-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Exelon Corporation *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Exelon Corporation *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, Exelon Corporation's trailing twelve months dividend is $1.62. Its dividend yield at its current price is 3.52%. Its current price is at a 9.2% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 13.2% premium to its 52 week low. If Exelon Corporation was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.99%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into Exelon Corporation stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,020 today. This total gain of $1,020 is generated by a capital gain of $713 and dividends of $307.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

Exelon Corporation's dividend has grown 5.7% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

Exelon Corporation operates as a holding company for regulated electric and gas utilities, primarily generating revenue through electricity transmission and distribution, which accounts for approximately 85% of total revenue. The largest revenue contributors are its PHI subsidiary, focused on Mid-Atlantic operations, and ComEd, serving northern Illinois, which together represent over 58% of the company's $24.3 billion full-year revenue. The fastest-growing segments include BGE, driven by Maryland distribution rates, and PECO, supported by Pennsylvania electric and gas rates and favorable weather conditions. These segments have shown significant year-over-year growth, reflecting the company's investments in infrastructure and regulatory recoveries.
